name: shell_run
description: Executes a specified shell command in the project's root directory or a specified subdirectory. IMPORTANT: This tool can execute arbitrary commands and has significant security implications. It should be used with extreme caution and ideally with commands that are pre-vetted or constructed carefully by the model. Output will include stdout and stderr of the command.

project_path.description: The root directory path of the project where the command should be executed.

command.description: The shell command to execute (e.g., 'npm install', 'python manage.py migrate').

working_directory.description: Optional subdirectory within project_path where the command should be run. Defaults to project_path itself.

env_vars.description: Optional key-value pairs for environment variables to set for the command's execution context.

hints.description: This tool is highly destructive and not idempotent as it can modify the file system, install packages, or run arbitrary code. Requires extreme caution and approval.
